page: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 48 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, alongside 256.0 GB of system memory. The CPU architecture is x86_64, featuring 48 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX, AVX-512, and AES cryptography. Storage is bundled as a 500 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% of tested servers, while multi-core performance falls in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show a strong cached read-only speed of 5450.04 GB/sec, but uncached large-block read and write speeds drop to average levels at 62.46 GB/sec and 63.91 GB/sec respectively. This indicates a performance tradeoff where cached operations are highly accelerated, but large-scale uncached memory access is limited to standard mid-tier speeds. Cryptographic and database operations perform strongly, with Redis SET operations reaching 68.18 million operations per second.

For machine learning workloads, the server demonstrates strong prompt processing speeds for small 135M models but exhibits weak performance when processing prompts for medium-sized 7B models. The hardware configuration offers a balanced resource density of 5.33 GB of RAM per core. This profile is suited for multi-threaded decompression tasks, static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from high core counts and strong single-core execution without requiring dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs at 2.0 GHz and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding 5.33 GB per core. It includes a 500 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core execution. While cached memory bandwidth is highly efficient, uncached large-block read and write speeds remain average. The server performs strongly in database operations, Redis SET transactions, and static web serving. However, it exhibits weak performance in prompt processing for medium-sized 7B LLM models. This configuration is suited for multi-threaded decompression, database management, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring high core density without GPU acceleration.

tagline: A balanced multi-core server featuring AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for database operations and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
